title: Role and RoleBinding for Pod Reader
description:
Create a ServiceAccount with read-only Pod access using a Role and RoleBinding.

Build the minimal RBAC chain: `monitor-sa` ServiceAccount → `pod-reader` Role (get/list/watch pods) → `RoleBinding` connecting them. Verify with `kubectl auth can-i` that the SA can list pods but cannot delete them.

**Common Pitfalls**: Use `apiGroups: [""]` for core resources like pods — not `"apps"`. A RoleBinding with the wrong namespace will not apply to the SA. Always verify with `kubectl auth can-i` after creating bindings.
steps: Create a ServiceAccount named monitor-sa in the default namespace
Create a Role named pod-reader in the default namespace with rules: apiGroups [""], resources ["pods"], verbs ["get", "list", "watch"]
Create a RoleBinding named monitor-binding in the default namespace that binds pod-reader to monitor-sa
Verify the SA can list pods: kubectl auth can-i list pods --as system:serviceaccount:default:monitor-sa
Verify the SA cannot delete pods: kubectl auth can-i delete pods --as system:serviceaccount:default:monitor-sa (should output 'no')
hints: Pods are in the core API group — use apiGroups: [""] (empty string), not "apps"
In the RoleBinding subjects block: kind: ServiceAccount, name: monitor-sa, namespace: default
roleRef.kind must be Role (not ClusterRole) since we're binding a namespace-scoped Role
kubectl auth can-i returns 'yes' or 'no' — use it to confirm before deploying real workloads

editor_config.initial_code:
# ServiceAccount
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: monitor-sa
  namespace: default
---
# Role: read-only pods
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: Role
metadata:
  name: pod-reader
  namespace: default
rules:
- apiGroups: # TODO: [""] for core API
  resources: # TODO: ["pods"]
  verbs: # TODO: ["get", "list", "watch"]
---
# RoleBinding: connect Role to ServiceAccount
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: RoleBinding
metadata:
  name: monitor-binding
  namespace: default
subjects:
- kind: ServiceAccount
  name: monitor-sa
  namespace: default
roleRef:
  kind: Role
  name: pod-reader
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io

_solution.code_solution:
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: monitor-sa
  namespace: default
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: Role
metadata:
  name: pod-reader
  namespace: default
rules:
- apiGroups: [""]
  resources: ["pods"]
  verbs: ["get", "list", "watch"]
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: RoleBinding
metadata:
  name: monitor-binding
  namespace: default
subjects:
- kind: ServiceAccount
  name: monitor-sa
  namespace: default
roleRef:
  kind: Role
  name: pod-reader
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
_solution.validation_script:
#!/bin/bash
set -e

kubectl apply -f solution.yaml
echo "✓ Resources applied"

# Verify ServiceAccount
kubectl get serviceaccount monitor-sa -n default > /dev/null 2>&1 && echo "✓ ServiceAccount monitor-sa exists" || { echo "✗ ServiceAccount monitor-sa not found"; exit 1; }

# Verify Role
kubectl get role pod-reader -n default > /dev/null 2>&1 && echo "✓ Role pod-reader exists" || { echo "✗ Role pod-reader not found"; exit 1; }

# Verify Role rules
verbs=$(kubectl get role pod-reader -n default -o jsonpath='{.rules[0].verbs[*]}')
if echo "$verbs" | grep -q "list" && echo "$verbs" | grep -q "get" && echo "$verbs" | grep -q "watch"; then
  echo "✓ Role includes get, list, watch verbs"
else
  echo "✗ Role verbs incomplete. Found: $verbs"
  exit 1
fi

resources=$(kubectl get role pod-reader -n default -o jsonpath='{.rules[0].resources[*]}')
if echo "$resources" | grep -q "pods"; then
  echo "✓ Role targets pods resource"
else
  echo "✗ Role resources do not include pods. Found: $resources"
  exit 1
fi

# Verify RoleBinding
kubectl get rolebinding monitor-binding -n default > /dev/null 2>&1 && echo "✓ RoleBinding monitor-binding exists" || { echo "✗ RoleBinding monitor-binding not found"; exit 1; }

# Test can list pods
can_list=$(kubectl auth can-i list pods --as system:serviceaccount:default:monitor-sa -n default)
if [ "$can_list" = "yes" ]; then
  echo "✓ monitor-sa CAN list pods"
else
  echo "✗ monitor-sa cannot list pods (got: $can_list)"
  exit 1
fi

# Test cannot delete pods
can_delete=$(kubectl auth can-i delete pods --as system:serviceaccount:default:monitor-sa -n default)
if [ "$can_delete" = "no" ]; then
  echo "✓ monitor-sa CANNOT delete pods (correct)"
else
  echo "✗ monitor-sa should NOT be able to delete pods (got: $can_delete)"
  exit 1
fi

echo "✓ All validations passed"
exit 0
